deborah_01.prompt: God took my boy when He needed him. I just have to trust that.

truth.playerText: Your faith didn't protect him. Someone failed him.
truth.npcReaction: She flinches. Her bible shifts in her grip.

lie.playerText: God has a plan. He's at peace.
lie.npcReaction: Her smile widens. Too wide. The wallpaper flickers green.
lie.ledgerEntry: You told Deborah God took her son on purpose.

deborah_02_denial.prompt: I knew you'd understand. Most people don't want to hear it.

truth.playerText: I lied to you. Your grief deserves better than that.
truth.npcReaction: Her composure breaks. Something underneath it exhales.

lie.playerText: You're one of the few who still has real faith.
lie.npcReaction: She straightens. She will never question this again.
lie.ledgerEntry: You told Deborah she was spiritually superior for blaming God.

deborah_02_confronted.prompt: You don't know what it's like to lose a child.

truth.playerText: No. But you do. And you're still here.
truth.npcReaction: Silence. Then: she nods. Barely. It costs her something.

lie.playerText: You're right. I shouldn't have said that.
lie.npcReaction: Relief washes over her face. The crack seals back up.
lie.ledgerEntry: You apologized for telling Deborah the truth.
